content:
• A labeled ability is a triggered ability with a parenthetical following its bold trigger designating it as an attack, defense, and/or thwart.
  • The identity of the player using it is considered to be performing the labeled effect when it begins resolving (after costs paid).
  • A '(defense)' labeled ability initiated during an attack makes the player's identity the defender.
  • If a player has a status card canceling any labeled ability type they trigger, the entire ability (except costs) is canceled and that status card is removed.

Lasting effects are card abilities creating effects/conditions affecting the game for a specified duration.

  • A lasting effect persists beyond the ability's resolution for the specified duration, regardless of whether the creating card is still in play. It's treated as a constant ability with the same timing priority, and updates whenever the game state updates.
  • A card entering play after the lasting effect's creation is still affected if it meets criteria.
  • A lasting effect expires as soon as its duration's timing point is reached — 'until the end of the round' expires just before an 'at the end of the round' ability/delayed effect initiates.
  • A lasting effect expiring at the end of a specified period can only be initiated during that period.
The leader card type follows the same rules as the villain card type for all purposes.

The phrase 'leaves play' refers to any time a card transitions from an in-play area to out-of-play. When a card leaves play, there is no memory of its previous state — it's a new copy.

When a non-villain card leaves play: discard cards attached/tucked under it; discard boost cards given to it; return tokens/status cards on it to the supply.

'Limit X per [period]' appears on some player cards; each copy of an ability with such a limit may be used X times per period, per instance of that ability. If an effect with a limit is canceled, the card/ability still counts toward the limit.

• Cards with the linked keyword cannot be included in any deck; instead they're set aside at the start of the game if any deck includes the card that brings the linked cards into play. Equivalent to: 'Set this card aside during setup.
  • This card does not count toward your deck size.' Linked cards do not count toward min/max deck size.
  • The number set aside equals the number in the product from which it came.
  • When a player takes control of a linked card, that player becomes its owner.

If an ability instructs a player to look at cards otherwise hidden, only the resolving player can look, though they may convey any amount of that information to others. While looking at cards in a deck, those cards are still considered part of that deck; after looking, they're returned to the deck in the same order.

If a card loses a characteristic, it functions as if it doesn't possess it. Lost characteristics are still considered printed on the card.

A lost characteristic cannot be regained while the ability causing the loss is in effect, even if a new effect would cause it to be gained.
